creator: Mr.DeaDSouL@hotmail.com
text:
I found this url is related: https://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-ports/2017-April/108075.html

I do have the same issue. I'm trying to compile gcc5.

I'm using new installation of FreeBSD-11-Release

unless the BOOTSTRAP is off, the gcc5 won't compile.



....
gmake[5]: Entering directory '/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/libcpp'
/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/./prev-gcc/xg++ -B/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/./prev-gcc/ -B/usr/local/x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0/bin/ -nostdinc++ -B/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/prev-x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0/libstdc++-v3/src/.libs -B/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/prev-x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0/libstdc++-v3/libsupc++/.libs  -isystem /usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/prev-x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0/libstdc++-v3/include/x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0  -isystem /usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/prev-x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0/libstdc++-v3/include  -isystem /usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libstdc++-v3/libsupc++ -L/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/prev-x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0/libstdc++-v3/src/.libs -L/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/.build/prev-x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.0/libstdc++-v3/libsupc++/.libs  -I/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libcpp -I. -I/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libcpp/../include -I/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libcpp/include -DLIBICONV_PLUG -g -O2 -gtoggle -W -Wall -Wno-narrowing -Wwrite-strings -Wmissing-format-attribute -pedantic -Wno-long-long  -fno-exceptions -fno-rtti -I/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libcpp -I. -I/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libcpp/../include -I/usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libcpp/include -DLIBICONV_PLUG  -c -o charset.o -MT charset.o -MMD -MP -MF .deps/charset.Tpo /usr/ports/lang/gcc5/work/gcc-5.4.0/libcpp/charset.c

                   ^

the ports I have compiled before trying to compile gcc5 are:

* ports-mgmt/pkg
* ports-mgmt/psearch
* ports-mgmt/portmaster
* sysutils/tmux
* sysutils/htop
* editors/vim

with almost all config options are on.


thanks.
